calculator.title: Forex Calculator
calculator.subtitle: Calculate position size, pip value, margin requirements, and potential profit or loss for any instrument before you open a trade.
calculator.cta_title: Ready to put your calculations into action?
calculator.cta_subtitle: Open a live or demo account and trade with the conditions you calculated.
calculator.guide_desc: Each calculator serves a specific purpose in the trade planning process. Use them together for a complete picture of any trade before opening it.

calc_guide.title: Position Size Calculator
calc_guide.output: The correct lot size that limits your loss to your chosen percentage if the stop loss is hit.
calc_guide.example: Account: $5,000 · Risk: 1% ($50) · Stop Loss: 20 pips on EUR/USD → Result: 0.25 lots
calc_guide.why: Oversizing is one of the most common reasons traders lose accounts quickly. A position size calculator enforces discipline by making risk consistent across all trades.

calc_guide.title: Pip Value Calculator
calc_guide.output: The exact USD value of 1 pip movement for your chosen instrument and lot size.
calc_guide.example: EUR/USD · 1.0 lot → 1 pip = $10.00 | EUR/USD · 0.1 lot → 1 pip = $1.00
calc_guide.why: Pip value varies by instrument and lot size. Knowing it precisely lets you calculate stop loss distances and take profit targets in dollar terms before entering a trade.

calc_guide.title: Margin Calculator
calc_guide.output: The amount of margin (collateral) required to open and maintain the position.
calc_guide.example: 1 lot EUR/USD · Leverage 1:100 → Required margin: $1,084 (at 1.08400 price)
calc_guide.why: Trading without knowing your margin requirement can result in unexpected margin calls. This calculator prevents overcommitting available margin across multiple open trades.

calc_guide.title: Profit / Loss Calculator
calc_guide.output: Estimated profit or loss in USD for the trade, based on price movement.
calc_guide.example: Buy 1 lot EUR/USD at 1.08400, close at 1.08700 → Profit: $300 (30 pips × $10/pip)
calc_guide.why: Visualising the profit/loss before entering a trade helps you decide whether the risk-to-reward ratio justifies the position — a core step in any trade planning process.

calculator.lots_desc: Lot size determines how many units of a currency or commodity you are buying or selling. Olla Trade supports micro lots from 0.01 — allowing precise position sizing for any account size.
calculator.lots_note: Pip values are approximate and vary by instrument and current exchange rate. Use the calculator above for precise values.

lot_sizes.name: Standard Lot
lot_sizes.notes: Full-size position. Suitable for larger accounts.

lot_sizes.name: Mini Lot
lot_sizes.notes: One-tenth of a standard lot. Good for medium accounts.

lot_sizes.name: Micro Lot
lot_sizes.notes: Smallest standard unit. Suitable for small accounts and learning.

calculator.risk_desc: The calculator is only one part of disciplined trading. These risk management principles help protect your capital across all market conditions.
calculator.risk_disclaimer: Trading calculators provide estimates based on inputs. Actual results may vary due to spreads, slippage, swap charges, and execution conditions. All trading involves risk of loss. Past performance is not indicative of future results.

risk_rules.desc: Most professional traders risk between 1% and 2% of account balance on any single trade. This allows 50–100 consecutive losses before depleting an account — providing time to recover.
For every $1 risked, target at least $2 in potential profit. A 1:2 risk-to-reward ratio means you only need to be right 34% of the time to break even over a series of trades.
Determine your stop loss price before opening any trade. This prevents emotional decision-making after the position is open and price moves against you.
Moving a stop loss further away from entry to avoid being stopped out increases your risk exposure. Only widen stops if your strategy explicitly calls for it.
Opening multiple positions in strongly correlated instruments (e.g. EUR/USD + GBP/USD) effectively multiplies your exposure. Account for this when calculating total risk.
Consistent application of position sizing calculators removes guesswork from trade sizing. Even experienced traders use them to maintain disciplined risk management.

calendar.title: Economic Calendar
calendar.subtitle: Track upcoming economic events, central bank announcements, and market-moving data releases to plan and manage your trading more effectively.
calendar.live_desc: Filter by impact level and currency. High-impact events (red) are the most market-moving — plan your trades accordingly.
calendar.how_desc: Each row in the economic calendar represents a scheduled data release or event. Understanding what each column means helps you interpret the information and assess its relevance to your trades.

how_cols.desc: Scheduled release time in server time (GMT+2 or GMT+3 depending on DST). Actual release may vary by a few minutes.
The currency most directly affected by the event (e.g. USD for US data, EUR for ECB events).
Expected market impact: High (red), Medium (yellow), Low (grey). Based on historical volatility and market significance.
Name of the economic release or event. Click for detailed description in the calendar.
The released figure — shown after the event fires. Green = beat forecast, red = missed forecast.
Consensus estimate from economists. The deviation between Actual and Forecast typically drives the market reaction.
The prior period's released value. Provides trend context when comparing with the new actual figure.

tips.t: Focus on the deviation, not just the number
tips.d: Markets react to how the actual figure differs from the forecast — not from the previous release. A CPI that is 0.3% above forecast moves markets more than one that is 0.3% above prior.
Review the day's high-impact events before your session opens. Know which pairs and instruments could see volatility and at what time.
US data affects not just USD pairs — it moves Gold (XAUUSD), US indices (US30, NAS100), and all major pairs simultaneously.
During and immediately after high-impact releases, spreads may widen significantly. Factor this into stop loss placement and trade sizing.

tips.t: Use the previous value for trend context
tips.d: A series of improving GDP or CPI readings can confirm a trend, giving greater weight to the current release and its market impact.

calendar.impact_desc: Each event is rated by its expected market impact. Use these ratings to prioritise which events to monitor and plan for.

impact_levels.desc: Events very likely to cause significant and rapid price movements. Requires active risk management.
Events that may cause moderate market movement, particularly for directly affected currency pairs.
Minor data releases unlikely to cause significant moves on their own, but worth noting in context.

calendar.events_desc: Understanding the purpose and market impact of key economic releases is essential for trading around news events effectively. Here are the most closely watched events.

major_events.name: Non-Farm Payrolls (NFP)
major_events.desc: The most market-moving US labour report. Measures net job creation outside the farming sector. A significant beat vs forecast typically strengthens USD; a miss weakens it.
major_events.tip: Stand aside or reduce exposure 30 min before release. Major slippage and gap risk.

major_events.name: Consumer Price Index (CPI)
major_events.desc: Primary inflation measure used by central banks to guide interest rate decisions. A hotter-than-expected CPI typically supports the base currency; a weak print may trigger selling.
major_events.tip: Watch the month-on-month figure — it captures the current trend better than year-on-year.

major_events.name: Interest Rate Decision
major_events.desc: The most significant scheduled event for each currency. The Fed, ECB, BoE, and BoJ set overnight lending rates. Rate changes or guidance create large, sustained directional moves.
major_events.tip: The press conference and statement wording often moves markets more than the rate decision itself.

major_events.name: Gross Domestic Product (GDP)
major_events.desc: Measures total economic output. Stronger growth supports the currency; contraction weighs on it. GDP preliminary readings move markets most — revisions tend to have smaller impact.
major_events.tip: GDP data is backward-looking. Forward guidance and PMI data often signal GDP trends in advance.

major_events.name: Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI)
major_events.desc: Forward-looking survey of business conditions. A reading above 50 signals expansion; below 50 signals contraction. Often used as a leading indicator for GDP and employment trends.
major_events.tip: Services PMI matters more than manufacturing PMI in most developed economies.

major_events.name: Retail Sales
major_events.desc: Measures consumer spending, which drives ~70% of US GDP. A strong print signals healthy consumer demand and supports the base currency. Weak prints may signal economic slowdown.
major_events.tip: Core retail sales (excluding autos) is watched more closely than the headline figure.

major_events.name: Central Bank Speeches
major_events.desc: Speeches from Federal Reserve governors, ECB officials, and other central bank members can shift market expectations. Hawkish comments support the currency; dovish comments weaken it.
major_events.tip: Speeches from Fed Chair and ECB President carry the most weight.

major_events.name: Unemployment Rate
major_events.desc: A key labour market indicator alongside NFP. Rising unemployment signals economic weakness; falling unemployment supports rate hike expectations.
major_events.tip: The participation rate provides important context — falling unemployment with falling participation can be misleading.

calendar.warning_text: During high-impact economic events, market prices can move rapidly and significantly within seconds of a data release. Spreads may widen substantially, slippage may occur, and stop-loss orders may be executed at prices different from those requested. Always ensure adequate risk management is in place before trading around major news releases. Economic calendar data is for informational purposes only and does not constitute investment advice.

alerts.title: Trading Alerts
alerts.subtitle: Set custom price and indicator alerts in MetaTrader 4 and receive notifications on desktop, mobile, or email — so you never miss a key market move.
alerts.setup_title: How to Set Up Alerts in MT4
alerts.risk_text: Trading alerts are a tool to help you monitor markets — they do not remove trading risk. Always apply appropriate risk management.
alerts.cta_subtitle: Open an account and access the full MT4 alert system at no extra cost.
alerts.overview_p1: Trading alerts are automated notifications that fire when a pre-set condition is met — such as a price reaching a specific level, a technical indicator crossing a threshold, or a time condition being triggered.
alerts.overview_p2: In MetaTrader 4, alerts are a built-in feature available on all platforms: desktop, WebTrader, iOS, and Android. They allow you to step away from your screens while staying informed about critical market levels.
alerts.overview_p3: Alerts are purely informational — they do not automatically execute trades. For automated trade execution, you would use an Expert Advisor (EA).

overview_cards.d: Set notifications at any price level
Trigger on MA crosses, RSI levels

alerts.types_desc: MetaTrader 4 supports several types of alert conditions to suit different monitoring needs.

alert_types.title: Price Level Alerts
alert_types.desc: Trigger a notification when an instrument's price reaches a specific bid or ask level. Ideal for monitoring key support, resistance, and breakout levels.

alert_types.title: Indicator Alerts
alert_types.desc: Set alerts based on technical indicator conditions — for example, when an MA crosses, RSI reaches a threshold, or Bollinger Bands are breached.

alert_types.title: Time-Based Alerts
alert_types.desc: Configure alerts to fire at a specific time — useful for reminding you of upcoming economic events or scheduled trading sessions.

alert_types.title: Margin Level Alerts
alert_types.desc: MT4 can alert you when your account's margin level falls to a specified percentage, helping you manage risk before automatic stop-out occurs.

delivery_channels.title: MT4 Desktop Popup
delivery_channels.desc: An on-screen popup appears in your MT4 desktop terminal when the alert condition is met. Available whenever MT4 is open and running.

delivery_channels.title: Mobile Push Notification
delivery_channels.desc: Receive a push notification on your iPhone or Android device via the MT4 mobile app — even when your computer is off.

delivery_channels.title: Email Notification
delivery_channels.desc: MT4 can send an email when an alert fires. Configure your email address in MT4 → Tools → Options → Email.

delivery_channels.title: Platform Sound Alert
delivery_channels.desc: Play a custom sound in MT4 when an alert triggers — useful for traders who are actively at their computer but monitoring other work.

steps: Open MetaTrader 4 on your computer.
Right-click any price level on a chart — select 'Set Alert'.
Configure the alert condition, type, and notification method.
Click 'Add' to activate the alert.
Manage all alerts via View → Terminal → Alerts tab.
Open the MT4 mobile app and navigate to a chart.
Tap and hold on the price level where you want an alert.
Select 'Set Alert' from the context menu.
Choose the alert condition and enable push notifications.
Ensure push notifications are enabled in your device settings for MT4.

use_cases.title: Breakout Monitoring
use_cases.desc: Set price alerts at key support and resistance levels. When price approaches or breaks through a level, receive an alert to assess whether to enter, exit, or adjust a position.

use_cases.title: News Event Preparation
use_cases.desc: Set a time alert before a major economic release to remind yourself to review your open positions, adjust stop losses, or stand aside during high-volatility periods.

use_cases.title: Risk Management Monitoring
use_cases.desc: Configure margin level alerts to notify you before your account reaches the margin call level, giving you time to add funds or reduce position size.

use_cases.title: Re-entry Setups
use_cases.desc: After missing an entry, set a price alert at your preferred entry level. When price returns, you receive a notification to reassess whether the setup is still valid.

alerts.faq_subtitle: Common questions about setting up and using trading alerts in MetaTrader 4.

vps.title: VPS Guide for MT4
vps.subtitle: A Virtual Private Server (VPS) allows your Expert Advisors to run 24 hours a day, 5 days a week — without needing your computer to stay on.
vps.what_title: A Dedicated Remote Computer for Trading
vps.benefits_title: Why Use a VPS for MT4?
vps.setup_title: Setting Up MT4 on a VPS
vps.cta_subtitle: Open an MT4 account and deploy your Expert Advisors on a VPS for uninterrupted operation.
vps.what_p1: A Virtual Private Server (VPS) is a remote computing environment hosted in a datacenter. It runs 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, independently of your personal computer. For forex traders, it provides a stable, always-on environment to run MetaTrader 4 and Expert Advisors.
vps.what_p2: Unlike your home computer, a VPS is unaffected by power cuts, internet outages, system restarts, or accidental shutdowns. Once you install MT4 on a VPS and configure your EAs, they will continue to execute without interruption.
vps.what_p3: You access and manage your VPS remotely from any device via Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP) — just as if you were sitting in front of a remote computer.

benefits.title: 24/5 Uninterrupted Operation
benefits.desc: Your EAs run continuously around the clock, even when your computer is off, ensuring no missed signals or execution gaps.

benefits.title: Low Latency Execution
benefits.desc: A VPS located close to trading servers can reduce order transmission time, which is important for time-sensitive strategies.

benefits.title: No Power / Internet Issues
benefits.desc: A hosted VPS is unaffected by your local power cuts, internet outages, or computer hardware failures.

benefits.title: Dedicated Resources
benefits.desc: VPS resources are dedicated to your use, unlike shared hosting, providing consistent performance for MT4 and your EAs.

benefits.title: Remote Access from Anywhere
benefits.desc: Connect to your VPS from any device with internet access. Manage your MT4 installation and EAs from your phone, tablet, or laptop.

benefits.title: Multiple Instances
benefits.desc: Run multiple MT4 installations with different accounts or strategies on a single VPS, subject to its resource capacity.

vps.infra_desc: A VPS hosted close to your broker's servers reduces the physical distance your orders must travel. The result is consistently lower latency — critical for time-sensitive trading strategies and Expert Advisor execution.

infra_items.label: 24/5 Uninterrupted MT4
infra_items.desc: Your EAs run continuously even when your computer is off, in sleep mode, or your internet is down.

infra_items.label: Close to Trading Servers
infra_items.desc: Choose a datacenter in the same region as your broker's infrastructure to minimise order transmission time.

infra_items.label: Remote Desktop Access
infra_items.desc: Manage your MT4 installation and EAs from any device via RDP — laptop, tablet, or smartphone.

infra_items.label: Multiple MT4 Instances
infra_items.desc: Run multiple MT4 accounts or strategies on a single VPS, subject to available server resources.

vps.infra_disclaimer: Olla Trade does not provide VPS services directly. The illustration above represents a typical VPS-to-server infrastructure concept. Clients are responsible for selecting and configuring their own VPS provider.
vps.specs_desc: The following specifications are recommended for running MetaTrader 4 with Expert Advisors. Requirements increase with the number of simultaneously running EAs and chart windows.
vps.specs_note: Olla Trade does not provide VPS services. These are general recommendations. Always verify VPS provider capabilities and suitability for your specific requirements.

specs.label: Location
specs.value: Choose a datacenter close to your broker's servers

setup_steps.d: Select a Windows-based VPS provider with a datacenter location close to your preferred trading region. Compare pricing, RAM, CPU, and included bandwidth.
Use Remote Desktop Connection (Windows) or Microsoft Remote Desktop (Mac/iOS) to connect to your VPS using the IP address, username, and password provided.
Open a browser on the VPS and download MetaTrader 4 from the official MetaQuotes website or your broker's download page. Install MT4 as you would on any Windows computer.

Open MT4 on the VPS, go to File → Login to Trade Account, and enter your Olla Trade account number, password, and select the correct server. Your account details were emailed to you upon registration.
Copy your EA files (.ex4 or .mq4) to the MT4 Experts folder (File → Open Data Folder → MQL4 → Experts). Restart MT4, then drag and attach each EA to the desired chart.
Click the AutoTrading button in MT4's toolbar (or press Ctrl+E) to allow EAs to trade. Verify the EA is running by checking the EA tab in the Terminal panel — it should show active.

security_items: Use a strong, unique password for your VPS and change it from the default
Enable Windows Firewall and keep it active
Keep Windows and all software up to date with security patches
Change the default RDP port (3389) to a non-standard port to reduce brute-force attacks
Consider enabling two-factor authentication if your VPS provider supports it
Do not install unnecessary software or browser extensions on your trading VPS
Take regular backups of your MT4 data folder (containing EAs, templates, and settings)
Disconnect and lock the session when not actively working on the VPS

faqs.q: How do I open a live account?
faqs.a: Click 'Open Account', complete the registration form (approximately 2 minutes), submit KYC documents, and deposit funds. Your account activates once verification is approved — typically within 1–2 business days.

faqs.q: What is the minimum deposit?
faqs.a: Standard: $10 · Pro: $2,000 · Elite: $20,000. These are the minimum amounts required to activate each account type and begin trading.

faqs.q: How long does a withdrawal take?
faqs.a: Withdrawal requests are processed within 24–48 business hours after approval. Crypto withdrawals are additionally subject to blockchain network confirmation times. Times may vary based on verification status.

faqs.q: How do I install MetaTrader 4?
faqs.a: Download MT4 from the Olla Trade website or MetaQuotes. Install on Windows (or use WebTrader). Log in with your account number, password, and the Olla Trade server address from your welcome email.

faqs.q: Why is my verification taking longer than expected?
faqs.a: Verification typically takes 1–2 business days. Delays may occur if documents are unclear or if the address doesn't match your registration details. Our team will contact you if more information is needed.

faqs.q: How do I contact Olla Trade support?
faqs.a: Email info@ollatrade.com or cst@ollatrade.com, or call +44 7418 641736. Our team is available 24/5, Monday to Friday.

faqs.q: Where can I view my transaction history?
faqs.a: Log in to your Olla Trade client portal and go to the Transaction History section. Filter by date range, type, or status to view all deposits, withdrawals, and account activity.

faqs.q: How do I become an affiliate?
faqs.a: Visit the Partner Programme page or email info@ollatrade.com with your details and traffic source. Our team will respond within 2–3 business days.

faqs.q: Can I withdraw the promotional credit?
faqs.a: No. Promotional trading credit cannot be withdrawn directly under any circumstances. It is applied as trading margin only. Net profits generated through your own trading activity may be withdrawable, subject to the specific promotion terms and Olla Trade's withdrawal policy.

faqs.q: Does accepting a bonus affect my withdrawals?
faqs.a: Promotional terms may include trading volume requirements before profits generated using the credit become eligible for withdrawal. Always read the complete terms of any promotion before participating.

faqs.q: Can I opt out of a promotion?
faqs.a: Yes. Participation in all Olla Trade promotions is entirely voluntary. If you wish to opt out of an active promotion, contact our support team.

faqs.q: Are promotions available to all clients?
faqs.a: No. Eligibility requirements vary by promotion. Generally, full KYC/AML verification is required. Some promotions are limited to new clients, specific account types, or eligible jurisdictions.

faqs.q: What happens to my credit if I close my account?
faqs.a: Promotional trading credit is forfeited upon account closure. It has no cash value and cannot be transferred or withdrawn.

complaints.title: Complaint Management
complaints.subtitle: Olla Trade takes all client complaints seriously. This page explains how to submit a complaint and what to expect from our review process.
complaints.info_title: What to Include in Your Complaint
complaints.hero_desc: Olla Trade takes all client complaints seriously. If you are dissatisfied with any aspect of our service, this page explains how to submit a complaint and what to expect from our review process.
complaints.process_desc: Follow these steps to ensure your complaint is handled quickly and thoroughly.

process_steps.title: Contact Support First
process_steps.desc: Many issues can be resolved quickly by our customer support team. Email cst@ollatrade.com or call +44 7418 641736 before submitting a formal complaint.

process_steps.title: Submit Formal Complaint
process_steps.desc: If unresolved, send a written complaint to complaints@ollatrade.com. Include your full name, account number, date of the incident, detailed description, and supporting documents.

process_steps.title: Acknowledgement
process_steps.desc: We will acknowledge your complaint in writing within 2 business days, providing a complaint reference number and the name of the person responsible for your case.

process_steps.title: Resolution
process_steps.desc: We aim to provide a final written response within 10 business days. Complex cases may take up to 30 business days. We will update you throughout the process.

review_items.title: Independent Review
review_items.desc: All complaints are reviewed by our compliance team, independent of the department involved. This ensures an impartial assessment of your concerns.

review_items.title: Full Investigation
review_items.desc: We investigate all aspects of your complaint, including reviewing account records, trade history, communications, and any documentation you provide.

review_items.title: Written Response
review_items.desc: You will receive a written response outlining our findings, the decision reached, and any remedial action taken. All decisions are communicated in plain language.

review_items.title: Additional Information
review_items.desc: We may contact you for further information during the investigation. Please respond promptly to avoid delays in the resolution process.

review_items.title: Record Keeping
review_items.desc: All complaint records are retained for a minimum of 5 years. Complaint data is used to improve our services and identify patterns or systemic issues.

review_items.title: Confidentiality
review_items.desc: Your complaint and all related correspondence are treated with strict confidentiality and handled in accordance with our Privacy Policy.

complaints.submit_desc: Email our complaints team directly at complaints@ollatrade.com. Include your account number and a clear description of your concerns. Alternatively, contact our support team first — many issues can be resolved without a formal complaint.
complaints.faq_subtitle: Common questions about our complaint handling process and timelines.

faqs.q: How long does it take to resolve a complaint?
faqs.a: We acknowledge all complaints within 2 business days of receipt. We aim to provide a full written response within 10 business days. For complex matters, resolution may take up to 30 business days. We will keep you informed of progress throughout the process.

faqs.q: What information do I need to include in my complaint?
faqs.a: Please include: your full name, registered email address, account number, date of the event, a clear description of the issue, and copies of any relevant supporting documentation such as screenshots or trade history.

faqs.q: Can I submit a complaint by phone?
faqs.a: We recommend submitting formal complaints in writing to complaints@ollatrade.com. You may also contact our support team by phone at +44 7418 641736 to discuss the matter informally before submitting a formal complaint.

faqs.q: What if I am not satisfied with the outcome?
faqs.a: If you are not satisfied with our final response, you may seek independent legal advice or escalate the matter to an appropriate external dispute resolution body. Details of escalation options are provided in our Complaint Management Policy.

faqs.q: Will submitting a complaint affect my account?
faqs.a: No. Submitting a complaint will not affect your account status, trading privileges, or any pending transactions. All complaints are reviewed independently and impartially by our compliance team.

complaints.legal_note: Olla Trade Ltd. is incorporated in Anguilla (Reg. No. A000001849) and operates as an execution-only trading service. All complaints are handled in accordance with our internal Complaint Management Policy. Complaint records are retained for a minimum of 5 years.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.subtitle: Your privacy is important to us. This policy explains how Olla Trade collects, uses, and protects your personal information.

sections.title: 1. Introduction
sections.content: Olla Trade Ltd. ('we', 'us', 'Company') is committed to protecting your privacy. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, store, and share your personal information when you use our services. This policy applies to all clients, visitors, and users of Olla Trade services. By using our services, you consent to the data practices described in this policy.

sections.title: 2. Information We Collect
sections.content: We collect personal information including: full name and date of birth; contact details (email address, phone number, residential address); government-issued identity documents and proof of address for KYC/AML purposes; financial information including account activity and transaction history; trading data and account preferences; device and browser information when you access our platforms; and any communications you send to us.

sections.title: 3. How We Use Your Information
sections.content: We use your personal information to: verify your identity and comply with KYC/AML regulatory obligations; create and manage your trading account; process deposits, withdrawals, and account transactions; provide customer support and respond to enquiries; send account-related communications; comply with legal and regulatory requirements; detect and prevent fraud, money laundering, and other financial crime; and improve our services and platform.

sections.title: 4. Sharing Your Information
sections.content: We do not sell your personal data to third parties. We may share your information with: identity verification service providers for KYC/AML compliance; payment processors to handle deposit and withdrawal transactions; regulatory authorities and law enforcement when required by applicable law; legal and professional advisors as necessary; and technology service providers who assist in operating our platform (subject to strict confidentiality obligations).

sections.title: 5. Data Security
sections.content: We implement industry-standard security measures to protect your personal information including: SSL/TLS encryption for data transmission; secure, access-controlled data storage; role-based access controls limiting data access to authorised personnel only; regular security assessments and audits; and incident response procedures. Despite these measures, no method of data transmission or storage is 100% secure.

sections.title: 6. Data Retention
sections.content: We retain your personal data for as long as your account is active and for a minimum of 5 years after account closure, as required by applicable anti-money laundering regulations. Transaction records are retained as required by financial regulations. You may request deletion of certain data subject to legal retention requirements.

sections.title: 7. Your Rights

risk.title: Risk Disclosures
risk.subtitle: Important information about the risks of trading Forex, CFDs, and other leveraged financial instruments. Please read carefully.
risk.warning_text: Trading Forex, CFDs, and other leveraged financial instruments carries a high level of risk and may not be suitable for all investors. It is possible to lose all of your invested capital. You should not invest money that you cannot afford to lose. Please ensure you fully understand the risks involved and seek independent advice if necessary. Past performance is not indicative of future results.

risk_categories.title: General Trading Risk
risk_categories.desc: Trading financial instruments including Forex, CFDs, metals, indices, and cryptocurrencies involves a high level of risk and may not be suitable for all investors. It is possible to lose all of your invested capital, or more than your initial deposit in some cases. You should only trade with funds you can afford to lose entirely. Past performance of any financial instrument is not indicative of future results.

risk_categories.title: Leverage and Margin Risk
risk_categories.desc: The use of leverage magnifies both potential gains and potential losses. Leveraged trading means a relatively small movement in the underlying asset can result in a disproportionately large impact on your account balance. At higher leverage levels, a small adverse price movement can eliminate your entire margin for a position. You must understand how leverage works and manage your exposure carefully.

risk_categories.title: Market Volatility Risk
risk_categories.desc: Financial markets can experience sudden, rapid, and significant price movements due to economic data releases, geopolitical events, central bank decisions, and other factors. During these volatile periods, losses can accumulate quickly and significantly. High volatility may also make it difficult to exit a position at your desired price.

risk_categories.title: Slippage and Execution Risk
risk_categories.desc: Slippage occurs when an order is executed at a price different from the price requested. This can happen during periods of high volatility, around major news events, or when market liquidity is low. Stop-loss orders do not guarantee execution at the specified price — in fast-moving markets, execution may occur at a significantly different price.

risk_categories.title: Liquidity Risk
risk_categories.desc: Some instruments may have limited liquidity at certain times — particularly outside regular market hours, around public holidays, or during market stress events. Low liquidity can result in wider spreads, difficulty entering or exiting positions at desired prices, and increased slippage. This risk is heightened for less commonly traded instruments.

risk_categories.title: Market Gap Risk
risk_categories.desc: Markets can 'gap' — meaning prices jump from one level to another without trading through intermediate prices. This typically occurs when markets reopen after closures (weekends, public holidays) to reflect news or events that occurred during the closure. Stop-loss orders may be executed at significantly different prices than specified during a gap event.

risk_categories.title: Technical and Platform Risk
risk_categories.desc: Trading is conducted via electronic platforms and internet connectivity. Technical failures including internet outages, platform downtime, hardware failures, and software issues can prevent you from accessing your account, monitoring positions, or executing orders. Olla Trade cannot guarantee continuous platform availability and is not responsible for losses resulting from technical issues outside our control.

risk_categories.title: Cryptocurrency CFD Risk
risk_categories.desc: Cryptocurrency markets are particularly volatile, largely unregulated, and can experience extreme price movements within very short periods. The value of cryptocurrencies can be highly sensitive to regulatory news, technological developments, market sentiment, and liquidity conditions. Crypto CFD trading is speculative and not appropriate for most retail investors.

checklist_items: Only trade with funds you can afford to lose in their entirety
Understand leverage and how it amplifies both profits and losses
Use stop-loss orders on all positions — and ensure you understand their limitations
Review your account margin level regularly and act before margin calls occur
Do not trade around major news events without understanding the volatility risk
Diversify across instruments rather than concentrating risk in single positions
Never trade based on tips, rumours, or unverified social media claims
Keep records of all your trades for performance review and tax purposes

vps.q: Do I need a VPS to trade?
vps.a: A VPS is not required to trade. However, if you use automated trading strategies (Expert Advisors), a VPS allows EAs to run continuously without keeping your computer on. It is particularly useful for traders who need uninterrupted 24/5 operation or who have unstable internet connections.

vps.q: What are the minimum recommended VPS specs for MT4?
vps.a: Minimum: 1GB RAM, 1 CPU core, 20GB SSD, Windows Server OS. For running multiple EAs: 2GB+ RAM, 2 CPU cores. The VPS should ideally be located geographically close to trading servers to minimise latency.

vps.q: Can I run multiple Expert Advisors on one VPS?
vps.a: Yes, provided the VPS has sufficient resources. Each MT4 instance running an EA consumes memory and CPU. Monitor resource usage to ensure your VPS is not overloaded, which could affect EA performance.

vps.q: Does Olla Trade provide VPS services?
vps.a: Olla Trade does not provide VPS services directly and does not endorse specific providers. There are many commercial providers available. The choice of provider is your responsibility.

vps.q: How do I connect to my VPS?
vps.a: Most Windows VPS providers use Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP). On Windows, use the built-in Remote Desktop Connection. On Mac, use Microsoft Remote Desktop. You will need the VPS IP address, username, and password from your provider.

alerts.q: Can I receive alerts on my mobile device?

No. Trading alerts are notifications only — they do not automatically open, close, or modify any position. An alert notifies you that a condition has been met; you must take manual action to execute a trade.

energies.q: What leverage is available on energy CFDs?
energies.a: Olla Trade offers leverage up to 1:100 on energy instruments. This amplifies both gains and losses in proportion to your position size.

crypto.a: Cryptocurrency CFDs allow you to speculate on the price of digital currencies without owning them. No wallet, no exchange account — trade directly from MT4.

crypto.q: Which cryptocurrencies are available on Olla Trade?
crypto.a: Olla Trade offers CFDs on BTC/USD (Bitcoin), ETH/USD (Ethereum), LTC/USD (Litecoin), and XRP/USD (Ripple).

crypto.q: Can I trade crypto without a digital wallet?
crypto.a: Yes. Olla Trade crypto CFDs do not require a digital wallet, exchange account, or actual cryptocurrency ownership.

crypto.q: What are the trading hours for crypto CFDs?
crypto.a: Olla Trade cryptocurrency CFDs are available Monday to Friday. Underlying crypto markets operate 24/7, so significant weekend price gaps may occur when CFDs reopen Monday.

crypto.q: What leverage is available on cryptocurrencies?
crypto.a: Olla Trade offers leverage up to 1:10 on cryptocurrency CFDs. Given high crypto volatility, strong risk management is essential.

stocks.a: A stock CFD allows you to speculate on the price of a company's share without owning the underlying stock.

stocks.q: Do I receive dividends on stock CFDs?
stocks.a: Stock CFD traders may receive dividend adjustments (credited or debited) when a company pays a dividend, depending on long or short position at the ex-dividend date.

stocks.q: What leverage is available on stock CFDs?
stocks.a: Olla Trade offers leverage up to 1:10 on stock CFDs. Conservative position sizing and stop-loss orders are strongly recommended.

stocks.q: Can I short a stock with Olla Trade?
stocks.a: Yes. With stock CFDs you can go short if you believe a company's share price will fall.

stocks.q: What hours can I trade stocks?
stocks.a: Stock CFD trading follows the hours of the underlying exchange (NYSE/NASDAQ for US stocks, LSE for UK stocks). Check your MT4 instrument specifications for current hours.

execution.a: Market execution means your orders are filled at the best available market price at the time the order is processed. Olla Trade uses market execution across all account types.

execution.q: Can slippage occur when trading with Olla Trade?
execution.a: Yes. Slippage can occur during periods of high market volatility, major economic news releases, or low liquidity conditions. Slippage can be positive (filled at a better price) or negative (filled at a worse price).

Olla Trade pricing is derived from aggregated quotes provided by our liquidity providers. The prices displayed in MetaTrader 4 reflect the best available bid and ask prices from our liquidity pool at any given moment.

Execution quality can be influenced by: current market volatility, available liquidity for the instrument being traded, the size of the order relative to available market depth, network connection latency, and the time of day and active trading sessions.

execution.q: What happens during volatile market conditions?
execution.a: During periods of high volatility — such as major economic data releases, central bank announcements, or significant geopolitical events — market prices can move rapidly and liquidity may temporarily reduce.

Yes. Spreads are variable and may widen significantly during periods of high market volatility, outside of regular market trading hours, around major economic news releases, or during periods of reduced market liquidity.

platform.a: MetaTrader 4 (MT4) is the world's most widely used retail trading platform, developed by MetaQuotes. It offers advanced charting, 30+ built-in technical indicators, 9 chart types, automated trading via Expert Advisors (EAs), and direct market access. Olla Trade provides MT4 access across desktop, web, iOS, and Android.

platform.q: Can I use MT4 on desktop and mobile?
platform.a: Yes. MT4 is available on Windows desktop (full-featured client), WebTrader (access from any modern browser with no installation required), iOS (App Store), and Android (Google Play). All versions connect to the same Olla Trade account in real time.

Yes. MetaTrader 4 has full support for automated trading through Expert Advisors written in MQL4. You can import custom EAs, use the built-in strategy tester to backtest against historical data, and run EAs live on your Olla Trade account.

platform.q: How do I log in to MT4 with my Olla Trade account?
platform.a: Your MT4 login credentials (account number, password, and server address) are sent to you by email when your Olla Trade account is approved. Open MT4, go to File > Login to Trade Account, enter your credentials, and select the Olla Trade server from the list.

MT4 Desktop for Windows is available for download from the Olla Trade website and from the official MetaQuotes website. The iOS app is on the Apple App Store and the Android version is on Google Play. WebTrader requires no download.

calendar.a: An economic calendar lists scheduled macroeconomic data releases, central bank announcements, and other market-moving events. Each entry shows the scheduled date and time, the country/region, the expected impact level (low, medium, high), and often forecast versus previous values.

calendar.q: How does high-impact news affect forex prices?
calendar.a: High-impact events — such as central bank rate decisions, Non-Farm Payrolls, CPI, and GDP releases — can cause significant rapid price movements. Markets often begin pricing in expectations before an event, then react sharply when actual data differs from forecasts.

calendar.q: Should I trade during major news events?
calendar.a: Trading around major news events carries elevated risk. Spreads may widen significantly, slippage can increase, and prices can move rapidly. If you trade around news, ensure you have appropriate risk management in place.

calendar.q: How are economic events classified by impact?
calendar.a: Events are classified as: Low Impact (unlikely to cause significant movement), Medium Impact (may cause some volatility for the affected currency), or High Impact (likely to cause significant and potentially rapid price movement).

calendar.q: Where can I find the economic calendar in MT4?
calendar.a: MetaTrader 4 does not include a built-in economic calendar. Access it through the Olla Trade website, through mql5.com/en/economic-calendar, or through third-party financial data providers. Many traders monitor the calendar in a separate browser tab while trading.

conditions.hero_subtitle: Competitive spreads, flexible leverage, and market execution across 500+ instruments. All conditions are disclosed upfront — no hidden fees, no surprises.
conditions.overview_desc: Olla Trade uses market execution across all accounts. Orders are filled at the best available market price at the time of submission, subject to available liquidity and market conditions.

overview_cards.title: Variable Spreads
overview_cards.body: Spreads are variable and reflect live market conditions. Major Forex pairs start from 0.0 pips on the Elite account and from 1.4 pips on the Standard account. Spreads may widen during major economic announcements, market open/close, or periods of reduced liquidity.

overview_cards.title: Market Execution
overview_cards.body: All orders are executed at the best available market price. No dealer intervention occurs in the order execution process. Expert Advisors (EAs) and automated trading strategies are fully supported on all account types.

overview_cards.title: Flexible Leverage
overview_cards.body: Leverage up to 1:500 is available on Forex pairs. Leverage varies by instrument class. Higher leverage increases both potential gains and losses. Clients should use leverage carefully and in accordance with their risk tolerance.

overview_cards.title: Negative Balance Protection
overview_cards.body: Olla Trade applies negative balance protection to all client accounts. In most market conditions, your losses will not exceed your deposited balance. Where an account falls into a negative balance due to exceptional market events, Olla Trade will reset the balance to zero at no charge.

overview_cards.title: No Requotes
overview_cards.body: The market execution model means orders are not subject to requotes. All orders are accepted and executed at prevailing market prices. Slippage — execution at a price different from the requested price — may occur in fast-moving markets.

overview_cards.title: EA & Scalping Allowed
overview_cards.body: Expert Advisors, scalping strategies, news trading, and hedging are permitted on all Olla Trade accounts. There are no restrictions on trading styles. All strategies must remain within the bounds of the account terms and conditions.

conditions.accounts_desc: Three account types designed for different experience levels and capital sizes — all on MetaTrader 4.
conditions.accounts_note: * Spreads are variable. Figures shown are indicative minimum spreads under normal market conditions. Actual spreads may be wider during low-liquidity periods or major news events.
conditions.spreads_note: Spreads are variable and depend on market liquidity. They may widen during low-liquidity sessions, major economic events, or market open and close periods.
conditions.leverage_desc1: Leverage allows traders to control a larger position with a smaller amount of capital. Olla Trade offers leverage up to 1:500 on Forex pairs. Leverage is not fixed — different instruments have different maximum leverage limits based on risk characteristics.
conditions.leverage_desc2: Higher leverage amplifies both potential profits and potential losses. Clients are responsible for maintaining sufficient margin in their accounts. Olla Trade does not guarantee that margin calls will always prevent losses exceeding the account balance under all market conditions.
conditions.margin_warning_body: When your account equity falls to 80% of the required margin (Margin Call level), you will receive a margin call notification. At 20% (Stop Out level), positions may begin to be closed automatically to protect remaining equity. Clients are responsible for monitoring margin levels at all times.

faqs.q: Which account is best for beginners?
faqs.a: The Standard account is ideal for beginners — $10 minimum deposit, no commission, full MT4 access, and 500+ instruments.

faqs.q: Can I upgrade my account type later?
faqs.a: Yes. You can upgrade from Standard to Pro or Elite by depositing the required minimum. Contact support to arrange your upgrade.

faqs.q: Is a demo account available?
faqs.a: Yes. A demo account is available for all account types so you can practise with virtual funds before going live.

faqs.q: What is the difference between variable and raw spreads?
faqs.a: Variable spreads fluctuate with market conditions. Raw spreads on the Elite account are the direct interbank rate plus a fixed commission per lot — typically lower in liquid market conditions.

faqs.q: How quickly can I open an account?
faqs.a: Registration takes about 2 minutes. KYC verification typically takes 1–2 business days. You can trade once approved.

execution.crosslink_text: Looking for the formal legal Order Execution Policy document?
execution.intro_p1: Olla Trade operates as an execution-only service using a market execution model. When you place a trade, your order is processed through our trading infrastructure and submitted to available liquidity at the best price achievable under current market conditions.
execution.intro_p2: Prices displayed in MetaTrader 4 are derived from aggregated quotes provided by our liquidity providers. These prices are variable and reflect real-time market conditions including supply and demand, overall market liquidity, and the time of the active trading session.
execution.intro_p3: Execution quality may vary depending on market conditions. Olla Trade does not provide investment advice and does not guarantee execution at any specific price. Orders are subject to available liquidity and prevailing market conditions at the time of execution.

The best available price is selected and the fill confirmation is returned to your terminal.

flow_features.label: Best Available Price
flow_features.desc: Our pricing engine aggregates quotes from multiple liquidity providers to achieve the best available bid and ask at time of execution.

flow_features.label: Execution Subject to Liquidity

flow_features.label: No Requotes

execution.model_desc: Understanding how different order types are processed. Execution quality may vary depending on market conditions and available liquidity.

model_cards.title: Market Execution
model_cards.desc: Market orders are filled at the best available price at the time of execution. The final price may differ from the quoted price at the moment the order was placed, particularly in fast-moving market conditions.

model_cards.title: Pending Orders
model_cards.desc: Pending orders instruct the platform to open a position when the market reaches a specified price. Types include Buy/Sell Limit and Buy/Sell Stop. Execution at the exact specified price is subject to available market liquidity.

model_cards.title: Stop Loss Orders
model_cards.desc: A Stop Loss order automatically closes a position at a specified loss level. Stop Loss orders help limit potential losses but do not guarantee closure at the exact requested price, particularly during rapid price movements or market gaps.

model_cards.title: Take Profit Orders
model_cards.desc: A Take Profit order closes a position automatically when the market reaches a specified profit target. Execution is subject to available market liquidity at the time the target price is reached.

model_cards.title: Slippage
model_cards.desc: Slippage refers to the difference between the expected price of a trade and the price at which the trade is actually executed. Slippage can occur in fast-moving or low-liquidity market conditions and can be positive or negative.

model_cards.title: No Requote Policy
model_cards.desc: Olla Trade does not issue requotes on client orders. Under the market execution model, all orders are accepted and executed at the best available prevailing market price at the time of submission.

execution.slippage_desc: Slippage is a normal and expected feature of trading in real financial markets. It occurs when the market moves between the moment you submit an order and the moment it is processed by our execution engine.

slippage_cards.title: When Slippage Occurs
slippage_cards.desc: Slippage is most common during high market volatility, major economic news releases, and periods of low liquidity — such as outside regular trading hours or around market open/close.

slippage_cards.title: Positive Slippage
slippage_cards.desc: Positive slippage occurs when your order is filled at a better price than requested — for example, a buy order fills lower than the requested price. This benefits the client.

slippage_cards.title: Negative Slippage
slippage_cards.desc: Negative slippage occurs when your order is filled at a worse price than requested. This can happen during rapid market movements or when large orders consume available liquidity at the requested price.

slippage_cards.title: Managing Slippage
slippage_cards.desc: Use limit orders instead of market orders where possible. Avoid trading immediately around major economic data releases. Monitor the economic calendar and apply appropriate position sizing.

execution.infra_desc: Olla Trade's trading infrastructure is built for reliable, continuous order execution across all instruments and sessions.

infra_cards.title: Server Location
infra_cards.desc: Trading servers are hosted in professional data centres with redundant connectivity and power systems for maximum uptime.

infra_cards.title: Liquidity Aggregation
infra_cards.desc: Prices are sourced from multiple institutional liquidity providers. The best available bid and ask prices are aggregated and presented to clients in real time.

infra_cards.title: MT4 Bridge
infra_cards.desc: Client orders placed via MetaTrader 4 are transmitted instantly through our MT4 bridge to our execution infrastructure for processing.

infra_cards.title: Continuous Monitoring
infra_cards.desc: Our operations team monitors execution quality, server performance, and connectivity on an ongoing basis during all trading sessions.

execution.pricing_desc: Olla Trade derives all prices from aggregated quotes provided by our institutional liquidity providers. Prices reflect real-time market conditions and are passed through to clients without any artificial mark-up beyond the disclosed spread.

execution.order_types_desc: MetaTrader 4 supports a range of order types to help you manage your trading positions. All order types are subject to prevailing market conditions and available liquidity.

order_types.name: Market Order
order_types.desc: Executed immediately at the best available current market price. The fill price may differ from the quoted price at the moment the order is placed.

order_types.name: Buy/Sell Limit
order_types.desc: Pending order to open at a more favourable price than current market. A Buy Limit opens below current price; a Sell Limit opens above.

order_types.name: Buy/Sell Stop
order_types.desc: Pending order to open beyond the current market level. A Buy Stop triggers above current price; a Sell Stop triggers below.

order_types.name: Stop Loss
order_types.desc: Automatically closes a losing position when the market reaches a specified level. Execution is not guaranteed at the exact price during rapid price movements.

order_types.name: Take Profit
order_types.desc: Automatically closes a profitable position when the market reaches a target price. Subject to market conditions and available liquidity at the time of execution.
